title: Harihar Fort
tagline: Harihar Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Harihar fort (also known as Harshagad) is a hillfort located about 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Nashik, 48 kilometres (30 mi) from Igatpuri, and 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Ghoti Budr
overview.description: Harihar fort (also known as Harshagad) is a hillfort located about 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Nashik, 48 kilometres (30 mi) from Igatpuri, and 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Ghoti Budruk, in the Nashik district of Maharashtra, India. It receives a high number of visitors due to its peculiar rock-cut steps.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Dugarwadi waterfall
topPlaces.description: Dugarwadi waterfall is 30 kilometres (19 mi) from Nashik and around 177 kilometers (110 mi) from Mumbai on Trimbakeshwar road and 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) from the Jawhaar road. In monsoon season, people are advised to take care as the water level suddenly rises.

topPlaces.name: Trymbakgad
topPlaces.description: Trymbakgad Fort/Brahmagiri Fort (Marathi: त्र्यंबकगड किल्ला) is a fort located 30 km from Nashik, in Nashik district, of Maharashtra, India. This is an important fort in Nashik district.

topPlaces.name: Manjira River
topPlaces.description: The Manjira river is a tributary of the river Godavari. It passes through the Indian states of Maharashtra, Karnataka and Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Trimbak
topPlaces.description: Trimbak (also known as Trimbakeshwar Trayambakēśvara) is a city and a municipal council in Nashik District in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The Trimbakeshwar Shiva Temple is located here, one of the twelve Jyotirlingas, where the Hindu genealogy registers at Trimbakeshwar, Maharashtra are kept.

topPlaces.name: Trimbakeshwar Temple
topPlaces.description: Trimbakeshwar Shiva Temple (श्री त्र्यंबकेश्वर ज्योतिर्लिंग मंदिर) is an ancient Hindu temple in the town of Trimbak, in the Trimbakeshwar tehsil, in the Nashik District of Maharashtra, India, 28 km from the city of Nashik and 40 km from Nashik road. It is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva and is one of the twelve jyotirlingas where the Hindu genealogy registers at Trimbakeshwar, Maharashtra are kept.

items.note: Dugarwadi waterfall is 30 kilometres (19 mi) from Nashik and around 177 kilometers (110 mi) from Mumbai on Trimbakeshwar road and 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) from the Jawhaar road. In monsoon season, people are advised to take care as the water level suddenly rises.
Trymbakgad Fort/Brahmagiri Fort (Marathi: त्र्यंबकगड किल्ला) is a fort located 30 km from Nashik, in Nashik district, of Maharashtra, India. This is an important fort in Nashik district.
The Manjira river is a tributary of the river Godavari. It passes through the Indian states of Maharashtra, Karnataka and Telangana.

Trimbak (also known as Trimbakeshwar Trayambakēśvara) is a city and a municipal council in Nashik District in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The Trimbakeshwar Shiva Temple is located here, one of the twelve Jyotirlingas, where the Hindu genealogy registers at Trimbakeshwar, Maharashtra are kept.

Trimbakeshwar Shiva Temple (श्री त्र्यंबकेश्वर ज्योतिर्लिंग मंदिर) is an ancient Hindu temple in the town of Trimbak, in the Trimbakeshwar tehsil, in the Nashik District of Maharashtra, India, 28 km from the city of Nashik and 40 km from Nashik road. It is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva and is one of the twelve jyotirlingas where the Hindu genealogy registers at Trimbakeshwar, Maharashtra are kept.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Harihar Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Harihar Fort is Apr – Jun, Sep – Oct. Summers are 10–22°C and winters are -6–10°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Harihar Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (98 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (39 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Harihar Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Harihar Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Harihar Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Harihar Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Harihar Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dugarwadi waterfall, Trymbakgad, Manjira River, Trimbak, Trimbakeshwar Temple.
